Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to act as an expert in process mining and data analysis I need to analyze the provided database schema process flow and event log data My goal is to 1 Identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors in the event log 2 Hypothesize why these anomalies might occur 3 Propose relevant SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses further

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets break down the normal process flow 1 Register Order Sales 2 Perform Credit Check Finance 3 Validate Stock Warehouse 4 Confirm Shipment Logistics 5 Ship Goods Logistics 6 Issue Invoice Finance 7 Receive Payment Finance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Analysis Follows the exact normal process flow No anomalies in sequence

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods occur before Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock Analysis Severe sequence violations Shipment confirmed and goods shipped before credit check and stock validation This is a major compliance and financial risk

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Ship Goods happens before Confirm Shipment Missing Validate Stock Confirm Shipment happens after Issue Invoice Analysis Sequence is highly irregular

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receive Payment occurs before Issue Invoice credit check stock and shipment Missing Credit Check and Validate Stock Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N but Ship Goods happens anyway Analysis Massive sequence and business logic violations

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets structure the response Identify Anomalies and Undesirable Behaviors Sequence Violations Out of Order Execution Missing Activities Skipped Steps Business Logic Attribute Anomalies Resource Department Anomalies

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesize Why These Anomalies Might Occur System Integration Errors Policy Violations Workarounds Employees bypassing controls to speed up priority orders Training Issues Staff unaware of the mandatory sequence System Overrides Manual Interventions Customer paid upfront causing the system or user to skip credit checks Data Entry Lags Users performing actions in the correct order but logging them in the system later causing timestamp inversions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Propose Relevant SQL Queries Query 1 Identify Sequence Violations Out of Order Use Window functions or self joins to compare timestamps of expected predecessor successor activities Query 2 Identify Missing Activities Use group by case id and having count distinct activity Query 3 Investigate Attribute Logic Anomalies Filter for specific additional info flags Query 4 Analyze Anomalies by Order Type or Resource Join with orders and resources

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait standard SQL is better than Postgres specific array operators if possible but since its Postgres it is fine Alternatively use conditional aggregation This is cleaner and more standard

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets review the prompt requirements Identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors Hypothesize why these anomalies might occur Propose relevant SQL queries to investigate further without any hints or guidance provided here

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Workarounds for Priority VIP Orders Case 1002 is a priority order Staff might be bypassing checks to meet SLAs Batch Data Entry Logging Lags Users do things physically in order but log them in the system later Upfront Payments Pre paid Orders Case 1004 might be a pre paid order System Integration API Asynchrony Automated systems might be pushing events asynchronously Negligence Lack of Training Simply forgetting to log Validate Stock or clicking Ship Goods before Confirm Shipment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SQL Queries Make sure they are PostgreSQL compatible Use CTEs for readability Include queries for identifying cases with missing mandatory activities identifying temporal sequence violations investigating business logic contradictions in additional info and correlating violations with order attributes and resource attributes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Reviewing the generated content against the prompt requirements Identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors Yes Hypothesize why Yes Propose SQL queries Yes Looks solid Proceeding to format the final output
